 {"id":"bd-12k","title":"Add console re-exports to sqlmodel prelude","description":"## Purpose
Add all necessary console types and traits to the sqlmodel prelude for easy access by users.

## Background
Users should be able to get started with:
use sqlmodel::prelude::*;

And have access to all commonly-used console types without additional imports.

## Implementation Details

### File Modifications
crates/sqlmodel/src/lib.rs
crates/sqlmodel/src/prelude.rs (create if not exists)

### Types to Re-export

#### Core Console Types
pub use sqlmodel_console::{
    SqlModelConsole,
    OutputMode,
    Theme,
    ConsoleAware,
};

#### Renderables (commonly used)
pub use sqlmodel_console::renderables::{
    ErrorPanel,
    QueryResultTable,
    SchemaTree,
    TableInfo,
    MigrationStatus,
};

#### Progress Components
pub use sqlmodel_console::progress::{
    OperationProgress,
    IndeterminateSpinner,
    BatchOperationTracker,
    PoolStatusDisplay,
};

### Conditional Export
Only export when console feature is enabled:
#[cfg(feature = "console")]
pub use sqlmodel_console::*;

### Prelude Module Structure
pub mod prelude {
    // Core types always available
    pub use crate::{Model, Session, Query, ...};

    // Console types when feature enabled
    #[cfg(feature = "console")]
    pub use crate::console::*;
}

## Verification Steps
1. use sqlmodel::prelude::* compiles with console feature
2. use sqlmodel::prelude::* compiles without console feature
3. All commonly-used types accessible
4. No naming conflicts with existing prelude items
5. Documentation shows available types

## Dependencies
- sqlmodel-console crate complete
- All renderable types implemented","acceptance_criteria":"Prelude re-exports SqlModelConsole when console feature enabled
Prelude re-exports OutputMode and Theme
Prelude re-exports all renderable types
Re-exports are feature-gated correctly
No naming conflicts with existing exports
All unit tests verify re-exports","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:14:23.840818971Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T11:04:24.255562683Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T11:04:24.255518700Z","close_reason":"Added console re-exports to sqlmodel prelude - compiles with and without console feature","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-12k","depends_on_id":"bd-318","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-12k","depends_on_id":"bd-vz2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":1,"issue_id":"bd-12k","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Required Unit Tests

1. test_prelude_exports_core_types - verify SqlModelConsole, OutputMode, Theme exported
2. test_prelude_exports_renderables - verify ErrorPanel, QueryResultTable, etc. exported
3. test_prelude_exports_progress - verify progress components exported
4. test_prelude_without_console_feature - verify compiles without console feature
5. test_prelude_no_naming_conflicts - verify no conflicts with existing prelude items
6. test_cfg_feature_gating - verify types only available when feature enabled
7. test_glob_import_usage - verify 'use sqlmodel::prelude::*' works correctly","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:30:17Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-18z","title":"Phase 9: Testing Infrastructure and Quality Assurance","description":"## Purpose
Establish comprehensive testing for all console components, ensuring correctness, agent compatibility, visual quality, and performance.

## Background
Testing console output requires special approaches:
- Unit tests for logic and structure
- Visual tests for rendered output
- Agent compatibility tests (parsing stdout/stderr)
- Performance tests (output must not slow operations)
- Feature flag tests (with/without console feature)

## Key Deliverables

### 1. Unit Test Suite
- Theme parsing and color generation
- OutputMode detection logic
- Renderable construction and configuration
- Progress calculation (rates, ETAs)
- Error message formatting

### 2. Visual Example Programs
- examples/console_demo.rs - showcase all renderables
- examples/error_showcase.rs - error panel variations
- examples/query_results.rs - table formatting
- examples/progress_demo.rs - progress indicators
- examples/schema_visualization.rs - tree and table info

### 3. Agent Compatibility Tests
- Verify stdout contains only semantic data
- Verify stderr contains decorative output
- Test plain mode produces parseable text
- Test agent detection via environment variables
- Verify no escape codes in plain mode

### 4. Performance Benchmarks
- Measure overhead of console output
- Compare with/without console enabled
- Benchmark progress bar update rate
- Ensure console does not become bottleneck

### 5. Feature Flag Matrix Tests
- Test compilation with all feature combinations
- Test runtime behavior with/without features
- Verify no compile errors in any combination

## Test Organization
tests/
  console/
    unit/
      theme_test.rs
      mode_detection_test.rs
      renderables_test.rs
    integration/
      agent_compat_test.rs
      stream_separation_test.rs
    visual/
      snapshot_test.rs

## Dependencies
- All console components complete (Phases 1-8)
- Example database for integration tests

## Verification
- All tests pass
- Code coverage > 80% for console crate
- Visual examples produce expected output
- Agent compatibility verified","acceptance_criteria":"Unit test suite has >80% code coverage
E2E test suite exercises all console features
Agent compatibility tests verify Plain mode behavior
Visual example programs demonstrate all features
Performance benchmarks measure console overhead
Test fixtures provide consistent sample data
All tests pass in CI environment","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:15:09.913125857Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T07:00:18.344986862Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T07:00:18.344860136Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-18z","depends_on_id":"bd-318","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-18z","depends_on_id":"bd-eqb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":2,"issue_id":"bd-18z","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Unit test suite with >80% coverage
- [ ] Visual example programs for all renderables
- [ ] Agent compatibility tests pass
- [ ] Performance benchmarks show acceptable overhead
- [ ] E2E test suite with detailed logging passes
- [ ] Test fixtures provide consistent sample data
- [ ] All tests run successfully in CI","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:37:36Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1a2","title":"Create sqlmodel-console crate directory structure","description":"# Create sqlmodel-console Crate Directory Structure

## Task Description

Create the directory structure for the new `sqlmodel-console` crate following the 
standard layout used by other crates in the workspace.

## Directory Structure to Create

```
crates/sqlmodel-console/
├── Cargo.toml
└── src/
    ├── lib.rs
    ├── mode.rs
    ├── console.rs
    ├── theme.rs
    ├── renderables/
    │   └── mod.rs
    └── widgets/
        └── mod.rs
```

## Implementation Steps

1. Create the `crates/sqlmodel-console/` directory
2. Create `crates/sqlmodel-console/src/` directory
3. Create `crates/sqlmodel-console/src/renderables/` directory
4. Create `crates/sqlmodel-console/src/widgets/` directory
5. Create placeholder files with minimal content (module declarations)

## Initial File Contents

### lib.rs (minimal)
```rust
//! SQLModel Console - Beautiful terminal output for sqlmodel_rust
//!
//! This crate provides styled console output that automatically adapts to
//! the terminal environment. When running under an AI coding agent, output
//! is plain text. When running interactively, output is richly formatted.

#![forbid(unsafe_code)]

pub mod mode;
pub mod console;
pub mod theme;
pub mod renderables;
pub mod widgets;
```

### mode.rs, console.rs, theme.rs (minimal)
```rust
//! [Module description]
// Implementation in subsequent tasks
```

### renderables/mod.rs, widgets/mod.rs (minimal)
```rust
//! [Module description]
// Renderables/widgets added in later phases
```

## Verification

```bash
ls -la crates/sqlmodel-console/
ls -la crates/sqlmodel-console/src/
ls -la crates/sqlmodel-console/src/renderables/
ls -la crates/sqlmodel-console/src/widgets/
```

## Notes

- Do NOT add to workspace Cargo.toml yet (separate task)
- Placeholder content is fine; real implementation comes in subsequent tasks
- Follow existing crate structure patterns from sqlmodel-core, sqlmodel-query, etc.","acceptance_criteria":"Directory crates/sqlmodel-console/ created
src/ subdirectory exists
src/lib.rs exists with basic structure
renderables/ subdirectory exists
widgets/ subdirectory exists
All directories have appropriate permissions","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:02:47.813952663Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T09:09:54.019349682Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T09:09:54.018573540Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["phase-1","rich-rust","setup"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1a2","depends_on_id":"bd-1vz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1lh","title":"Implement BatchOperationTracker for bulk database operations","description":"## Purpose
Create a specialized tracker for bulk database operations that tracks batches, rows affected, errors, and calculates rates automatically.

## Background
Bulk operations like batch inserts, updates, or data migrations have specific tracking needs:
- Batch-level progress (batch 5 of 20)
- Row-level progress (2500 rows inserted)
- Error counting and threshold warnings
- Automatic rate calculation
- Summary statistics at completion

## Implementation Details

### File Location
crates/sqlmodel-console/src/renderables/batch_tracker.rs

### Core Struct
BatchOperationTracker holds:
- operation_name: String
- total_batches: u64
- completed_batches: u64
- total_rows: u64
- processed_rows: u64
- error_count: u64
- started_at: Instant
- batch_times: Vec<Duration> (for rate smoothing)
- theme: Theme

### Rendering (Rich Mode)
Two-line display:
Line 1: Batch progress bar
Line 2: Row count, rate, errors

Batch insert [=========>        ] 50% (10/20 batches)
  Rows: 5,000/10,000 | Rate: 523 rows/s | Errors: 0

### Rate Calculation
- Track last N batch durations for smoothed rate
- rows_per_second = rows_in_recent_batches / duration_of_recent_batches
- Avoids spiky rates from single fast/slow batches

### Error Highlighting
- 0 errors: green or hidden
- 1+ errors: yellow with count
- Errors > threshold (configurable): red with warning

### Plain Text (Agent Mode)
Batch insert: 50% (10/20 batches), 5000/10000 rows, 523 rows/s, 0 errors

### Completion Summary
When all batches complete, show summary panel:
- Total time
- Total rows
- Average rate
- Error count
- Success rate percentage

## API Design
BatchOperationTracker::new(name, total_batches, total_rows)
  .theme(theme)
  .error_threshold(10)  // warn when errors exceed this
  .complete_batch(rows_in_batch)
  .record_error()
  .render(width) / .render_plain()
  .render_summary()  // final summary

## Verification Steps
1. Test normal batch completion
2. Test with errors
3. Test error threshold warning
4. Verify rate calculation accuracy
5. Test completion summary
6. Verify plain text format
7. Test with single batch (edge case)

## Dependencies
- OperationProgress (reuse progress bar logic)
- Theme from this crate
- std::time for timing","acceptance_criteria":"BatchOperationTracker shows overall batch progress
Tracker shows individual item status within batch
Tracker handles cancellation gracefully
Plain mode outputs batch status updates
All unit tests verify batch tracking
Performance tests confirm acceptable overhead for large batches","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:12:52.556586848Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T11:28:47.633125588Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T11:28:47.633082677Z","close_reason":"Implemented BatchOperationTracker with rate smoothing, error tracking, and 26 unit tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1lh","depends_on_id":"bd-1q2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1lh","depends_on_id":"bd-3gj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":3,"issue_id":"bd-1lh","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Unit Tests to Implement

1. test_batch_tracker_creation - verify initial state
2. test_batch_complete - verify batch counting
3. test_batch_rows_tracking - verify row accumulation
4. test_batch_rate_calculation - verify throughput
5. test_batch_error_recording - verify error count
6. test_batch_error_threshold - verify warning trigger
7. test_batch_render_plain - verify text format
8. test_batch_summary - verify completion summary
9. test_batch_single_batch - edge case
10. test_batch_many_batches - stress test","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:27:08Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1lv","title":"Implement PoolStatusDisplay dashboard renderable","description":"## Purpose
Create a visual dashboard renderable that displays connection pool status at a glance, showing pool utilization, health, and queue status.

## Background
Connection pools are critical infrastructure. Operators need to quickly assess:
- How many connections are active vs idle
- Whether the pool is under pressure (requests waiting)
- Health metrics (timeouts, errors)
- Configuration (min/max connections)

## Implementation Details

### File Location
crates/sqlmodel-console/src/renderables/pool_status.rs

### Core Struct
PoolStatusDisplay struct holds:
- stats: PoolStats (from sqlmodel-pool)
- theme: Theme
- width: Option<usize>
- show_history: bool (show recent utilization trend)

### PoolStats (expected interface from sqlmodel-pool)
struct PoolStats {
    active_connections: u32,
    idle_connections: u32,
    waiting_requests: u32,
    max_connections: u32,
    min_connections: u32,
    total_acquired: u64,
    total_released: u64,
    avg_acquisition_time_ms: f64,
    pool_created_at: Instant,
}

### Visual Layout (Rich Mode)


### Health Status Logic
- HEALTHY: waiting=0, active < 80% of max
- BUSY: waiting=0, active >= 80% of max  
- DEGRADED: waiting > 0 but < max
- EXHAUSTED: waiting >= max or active = max for extended period

### Color Coding
- Healthy: green
- Busy: yellow
- Degraded: orange
- Exhausted: red

### Plain Text (Agent Mode)
Pool: 8/20 active (40%), 0 waiting, HEALTHY
  Active: 5, Idle: 3, Max: 20
  Avg acquire: 2.3ms, Uptime: 2h 15m

## API Design
PoolStatusDisplay::new(stats: &PoolStats)
  .theme(theme)
  .width(80)
  .show_history(true)
  .render(width) / .render_plain()

## Verification Steps
1. Test with healthy pool (low utilization)
2. Test with busy pool (high utilization)
3. Test with exhausted pool (waiting > 0)
4. Verify bar chart renders correctly at various widths
5. Verify plain text output
6. Test uptime formatting
7. Test with zero stats (fresh pool)

## Dependencies
- sqlmodel-pool for PoolStats interface
- rich_rust Panel, ProgressBar components
- Theme from this crate","acceptance_criteria":"PoolStatusDisplay shows active/idle/max connections
Display updates in real-time during pool activity
Display shows connection acquisition queue depth
Plain mode outputs status line format
All unit tests verify rendering
Performance tests confirm low overhead","notes":"PoolStatusDisplay dashboard fully implemented with PoolHealth enum, PoolStatsProvider trait, render_plain()/render_styled() methods, 23 tests passing, all quality gates pass","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:12:10.255536895Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T10:53:10.887703018Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T10:53:10.887618990Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1lv","depends_on_id":"bd-1q2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1lv","depends_on_id":"bd-1rn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":4,"issue_id":"bd-1lv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Unit Tests to Implement

1. test_pool_status_creation - verify construction
2. test_pool_status_healthy - verify healthy state styling
3. test_pool_status_busy - verify busy state styling
4. test_pool_status_degraded - verify degraded state styling
5. test_pool_status_exhausted - verify exhausted state styling
6. test_pool_utilization_bar - verify bar rendering
7. test_pool_render_plain - verify text format
8. test_pool_render_rich - verify panel output
9. test_pool_stats_formatting - verify number formatting
10. test_pool_uptime_display - verify duration formatting

Use MockPool from fixtures (bd-1pw).","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:27:53Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1m0","title":"Write comprehensive rustdoc for sqlmodel-console API","description":"## Purpose
Add thorough rustdoc documentation to all public types, traits, and functions in sqlmodel-console crate.

## Background
Rustdoc is the primary API reference for Rust libraries. Every public item needs:
- Purpose description
- Usage examples
- Parameter documentation
- Return value documentation
- Links to related items

## Implementation Details

### Documentation Scope
All public items in:
- lib.rs (crate overview, feature flags)
- console.rs (SqlModelConsole, OutputMode)
- theme.rs (Theme, color definitions)
- All renderables (ErrorPanel, QueryResultTable, etc.)
- Progress components
- Traits (ConsoleAware, etc.)

### Documentation Style

#### Module-level
//! # sqlmodel-console
//!
//! Rich terminal output for SQLModel Rust operations.
//!
//! ## Quick Start
//! (code example)
//!
//! ## Features
//! - Agent-safe output (auto-detects AI coding agents)
//! - Rich renderables (tables, panels, trees)
//! - Themeable colors
//!
//! ## Feature Flags
//! - console (default): Enable console output
//! - syntax: Enable SQL syntax highlighting

#### Struct Documentation
/// A styled error panel for displaying sqlmodel errors.
///
/// ErrorPanel renders errors with:
/// - Color-coded severity
/// - Error context and location
/// - Suggestions when available
///
/// # Examples
///
/// Basic usage:
/// (code example)
///
/// With custom theme:
/// (code example)
///
/// # Agent Compatibility
/// In plain mode, renders as: ERROR: message (code)
pub struct ErrorPanel { ... }

#### Method Documentation
/// Create a new error panel from an Error.
///
/// # Arguments
/// * error - The sqlmodel error to display
///
/// # Returns
/// A new ErrorPanel ready for rendering
///
/// # Example
/// (code example)
pub fn new(error: &Error) -> Self { ... }

### Cross-References
Use intra-doc links:
/// See also: [Theme::error_color]
/// Related: [QueryResultTable] for displaying results

### Feature-Gated Documentation
#[cfg_attr(doc, doc = "Requires feature: syntax")]
#[cfg(feature = "syntax")]
pub struct DdlDisplay { ... }

### Examples in Docs
All examples should:
- Compile (cargo test --doc)
- Be minimal but complete
- Show both rich and plain output where relevant

## Verification Steps
1. cargo doc builds without warnings
2. cargo test --doc passes
3. All public items have documentation
4. Examples are runnable
5. Cross-references resolve
6. Feature flags documented

## Dependencies
- All console APIs finalized
- Code examples working","acceptance_criteria":"All public types have rustdoc comments
All public functions have rustdoc comments with examples
Module-level documentation explains purpose
Examples in docs compile and run (doctests)
cargo doc generates without warnings
Documentation follows Rust API guidelines","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:16:58.274332388Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T06:56:17.692485588Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T06:56:17.692361477Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1m0","depends_on_id":"bd-2e8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1m0","depends_on_id":"bd-2sh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:53:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
